---
title: "BIOL 275 Lab 4 Graphing with ggplot"
format: typst
output-file: lab-04-worksheet.pdf
---

1.  In your own words, what does it mean to “build” a graph in ggplot2 rather than choosing a graph type?

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 1.5in)
```
2.  What dataset is being used in this example? 

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 0.7in)
```

3.  Which variables are mapped to the x- and y-axes in this plot? 

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 0.7in)
```

4.  In this lab, what type of geometry is used to create scatterplots? Give the exact function name. 

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 0.7in)
```

5.  Do you need to manually define scales in order to make a useful plot? Why or why not? 

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 2in)
#pagebreak()
```

6.  What changes and what stays the same when a plot is faceted. 

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 2in)
```

7.  List the variables you used for the x and y axes, for color, and for facetting in the gapminder scatterplot. 

```{=typst}
#answerbox(height: 3in)
```
